title: alpinia alba
intro: Alpinia alba, also known as White Ginger or Lesser Galangal, is a type of rhizomatous perennial plant native to tropical Asia, characterized by its white, fragrant, and edible rhizomes. Alpinia alba has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Alpinia alba include reducing pain and inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Alpinia alba can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
